https://jashort.github.io/projects/bmous.body:
A minimal project to control lighting and scroll wheel mode on Razer Basilisk V3 mice via direct USB HID, without needing Razer drivers or Synapse.
View Source
Features showcase
Set a solid color bmouse static ff0000
Single-color breathing bmouse breathe 00ff00
Two-color breathing bmouse breathe-dual ff0000 0000ff
Rainbow spectrum cycling bmouse spectrum
Light up on click bmouse reactive ff0000 --speed 1
Turn LEDs off bmouse off
Get or set brightness bmouse brightness 200
Get or set scroll wheel mode bmouse scroll
Why?
Razer’s official software, Synapse, is resource-heavy and only available for Windows and macOS.
OpenRazer provides Linux support but requires a kernel driver and user-space daemon, which can be overkill if you just want to control lighting without installing additional software or drivers.
Some features like smart-reel can only be configured via software
https://jashort.github.io/projects/bmous.description: Razer Basilisk V3 mouse control CLI without Synapse or OpenRazer
https://jashort.github.io/projects/bmous.title: BMouse
